title: High Protein Vegetarian Lentil Buddha Bowl
metaDescription: Vegetarian lentil buddha bowl with 28g protein per serving — red lentils, roasted vegetables, and a tahini dressing. No meat, no protein powder.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: vegetarian buddha bowl, lentil bowl high protein, meatless protein bowl

steps.name: Roast the vegetables
steps.text: Preheat oven to 400°F. Toss sweet potato cubes and chickpeas with olive oil, cumin, salt, and pepper. Roast for 22-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until the sweet potato is tender and the chickpeas are slightly crisp.

steps.name: Cook the lentils
steps.text: While the vegetables roast, combine red lentils and vegetable broth in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 15-18 minutes, until the lentils are tender and most liquid is absorbed.

steps.name: Make the dressing
steps.text: Whisk tahini, lemon juice, and 2-4 tbsp water together until smooth and pourable. Season with a pinch of salt.

steps.name: Assemble the bowls
steps.text: Divide spinach among four bowls. Top with warm lentils, roasted sweet potato, and chickpeas. Drizzle with tahini dressing before serving.

tips.question: Can I meal prep this bowl in advance?
tips.answer: Yes — the lentils and roasted vegetables both keep well in the fridge for up to 4 days. Store the tahini dressing separately and add fresh spinach just before eating so it doesn't wilt.

tips.question: Why red lentils instead of green or brown?
tips.answer: Red lentils cook faster and break down softer, which gives this bowl a creamier texture. Green or brown lentils work too, but need about 10 extra minutes of simmering and hold their shape more.

tips.question: How do I add more protein without meat?
tips.answer: Stir in 1/2 cup of cooked quinoa or top with a soft-boiled egg if you're not fully plant-based — both add 6-8g of additional protein per serving.

tips.question: Is this recipe vegan?
tips.answer: Yes, as written it's fully vegan — just double check your vegetable broth doesn't contain any hidden animal-derived ingredients.

storyIntro: Vegetarian protein bowls usually lean on tofu or a scoop of protein powder blended into something, but red lentils quietly do the job just as well without either. A cup of dried lentils turns into a full pot of protein-dense base that tastes like it took way longer than 40 minutes.
Roasting the chickpeas alongside the sweet potato adds a second protein layer with actual texture — crisp edges instead of another soft, mushy component in the bowl.
storyNote: Simmer the lentils uncovered — covering the pot traps steam and can make them gluey instead of tender and separate.

images.heroAlt: Buddha bowl with red lentils, roasted sweet potato, chickpeas, spinach and tahini dressing
images.textureAlt: Close-up spoon lifting red lentils showing creamy texture next to roasted sweet potato cubes
